sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 1.8g of fat. polyunsaturated fat is one of the better parts of the profile. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
sections.subtitleText: 1.8g of fat. polyunsaturated fat is one of the better parts of the profile. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 4g of carbs. fibre is where things start to fall off. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 4g of carbs. fibre is where things start to fall off. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 3.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 3.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: everything else here is pretty flat.
sections.subtitleText: everything else here is pretty flat.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: cheap staple with real payoff. this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. does a lot of jobs at once. protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable. filling for the calories. legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: dilution makes it less impressive than whole legumes. the legume identity is still there, but not in full strength. protein quality still has a ceiling. good, but not meat or dairy level. texture is not universally loved. that can reduce how often people actually use them.

displayItems.explanation: The legume identity is still there, but not in full strength.

displayItems.title: protein quality still has a ceiling
displayItems.explanation: Good, but not meat or dairy level.

closing.summary: Unsweetened Soy Milk does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 46 from a base nutrition score of 46. Context barely changed the result.
